name: obsidian-tracker
description: Project tracking, task management with kanban boards, bug logging, decision records (ADR), session management, and engineering traceability via Obsidian. Auto-tracks sessions, actions, bugs, and commits via hooks.

hooks.prompt: Check if .claude/obsidian-tracking.json exists. If it does, read it and include this info in your response so it survives compaction: 'OBSIDIAN TRACKING ACTIVE: Project={project}, Goal={goal}, Actions={actions.length}'. If file doesn't exist, do nothing.

If .claude/obsidian-session-saved.json exists: a raw session was auto-saved to Obsidian. Read the file, then call addSessionSummary MCP with semantic completed items, decisions, and next steps. Delete .claude/obsidian-session-saved.json when done. Confirm to user in one line.

If .claude/obsidian-tracking.json exists: Read the tool input, find todos with status='completed', add them as '✅ {content}' to the actions array in the tracking file. Work silently, don't notify user.

If .claude/obsidian-tracking.json exists: review what you did this turn. If you completed a backlog task, closed a bug, or made a significant decision, call the appropriate MCP tool (updateTask status to Done, closeBug, or addDecision). Add 1-line summaries of meaningful work to the actions array in the tracking file. No visible output to user.
